source.title: Saliency, switching, attention and control: a network model of insula function

confidence_rationale: Canonical review establishing salience network (AI + dACC) as a switching hub between large-scale networks, with right AI as causal outflow node. Temporal precedence data (AI activity precedes CEN/DMN transitions) supports event-triggered discrete reconfiguration rather than continuous modulation. Mapping is strong for the network-switch characterisation of MECH-259; weaker for the specific threshold/hysteresis implementation. Source quality is high (>2000 citations).
failure_signatures: The paper does not characterise the transition as explicitly discrete vs graded; threshold mechanics are not directly tested.
The switching model is described at a network level; a purely graded implementation of the same network could still be consistent with the Menon & Uddin findings.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The right anterior insula plays a critical and causal role in switching between the central executive network and the default mode network; salience filters at multiple levels funnel salient events to the AI, which then orchestrates network reconfiguration.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-259 posits that the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) fires a whole-system operating_mode switch when precision-weighted salience exceeds a threshold. Menon & Uddin ground the switch-not-modulate characterisation: the AI/dACC node transitions the system to a new attentional configuration, consistent with discrete operating_mode change rather than smooth policy update.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Menon & Uddin describe network-level transitions (DMN vs CEN) not a general operating_mode vector. The jump from 'two-network toggle' to 'multi-mode coordinator with Schmitt-trigger hysteresis' is an architectural extrapolation not directly validated by this paper. Transfer risk is low for the 'switch' characterisation but higher for the specific threshold mechanism.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI + Granger causality; multiple cognitive tasks and stimulus modalities; healthy adults.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (canonical review, extensively replicated). Mapping fidelity penalised because the paper does not test discrete-vs-graded directly; it is consistent with discrete switching but does not rule out graded modulation.
